title: [CI:DOCS] Configure Renovate
body:
[![Mend Renovate](https://app.renovatebot.com/images/banner.svg)](https://renovatebot.com) Welcome to [Renovate](https://togithub.com/renovatebot/renovate)! This is an onboarding PR to help you understand and configure settings before regular Pull Requests begin. 🚦 To activate Renovate, merge this Pull Request. To disable Renovate, simply close this Pull Request unmerged. --- ### Detected Package Files * `contrib/hello/Containerfile` (dockerfile) * `contrib/podmanimage/stable/Containerfile` (dockerfile) * `contrib/podmanimage/testing/Containerfile` (dockerfile) * `contrib/podmanimage/upstream/Containerfile` (dockerfile) * `contrib/podmanremoteimage/Containerfile` (dockerfile) * `.github/workflows/check_cirrus_cron.yml` (github-actions) * `.github/workflows/issue-labeler.yml` (github-actions) * `.github/workflows/labeler.yml` (github-actions) * `.github/workflows/pr-title.yml` (github-actions) * `.github/workflows/rerun_cirrus_cron.yml` (github-actions) * `.github/workflows/stale.yml` (github-actions) * `.github/workflows/upload-win-installer.yml` (github-actions) * `go.mod` (gomod) * `docs/requirements.txt` (pip_requirements) * `.cirrus.yml` (regex) ### Configuration 🔡 Renovate has detected a custom config for this PR. Feel free to ask for [help](https://togithub.com/renovatebot/renovate/discussions) if you have any doubts and would like it reviewed. Important: Now that this branch is edited, Renovate can't rebase it from the base branch any more. If you make changes to the base branch that could impact this onboarding PR, please merge them manually. ### What to Expect With your current configuration, Renovate will create 21 Pull Requests:

comments.body: @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL at the expected list of dep. update. PRs.  Besides the github-actions PRs (expected), the golang updates are much more extensive than the currently open Dependabot PRs.  Many of them are digest-updates (not supported by Dependabot?).  I currently have Renovate set to propose digest-updates at most, once per month.  What's your opinion, too much, too little, or something else?

>  I currently have Renovate set to propose digest-updates at most, once per month. What's your opinion, too much, too little, or something else?

Can we filter this per repo? Ideally all containers repos should be updated daily (e.g. common, image, storage, buildah), we encounter regressions often that could have been caught much earlier if we do that.
For other repos once per month should be good IMO.

Just to clarify: Regular pinned version or version-range updates happen immediately, though PRs will only open at a rate of one-per-hour (to not overwhelm CI).  The monthly schedule I have set, is just for digest-based updates.  They're limited b/c otherwise we'd be bombarded with update PRs with every dependency commit.

> Can we filter this per repo?

Yep, we sure can.  What kind of rate/schedule is more appropriate?  Once per week maybe?

I think we can discus this at cabal, but IMO containers repo updates should happen daily to notice as soon as possible if something breaks. I think we agreed on, that people should also vendor their changes immediately into podman to make sure that it does not break anything but this clearly does not happen for whatever reasons.
The easiest way to fix this is to just let the automation take care of this. I or someone else will review the Podman PRs quickly and then see that some tests failed, then I only need to look at the change set from one day and can easily ping the person who I suspect caused the problem.

The current process is clearly not working properly, see just yesterday we cut a c/storage release without testing it in podman before: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18060

Summary of cabal decisions:

- Renovate should check for digest-based updates daily (for containers-repo. deps)
- Renovate should NOT automatically rebase open PRs
- For c/image, c/common, and c/storage updates, they should be grouped together in a single PR.

> Renovate should check for digest-based updates daily.

Only for containers dependencies, other deps will be to much IMO.

> Only for containers dependencies

I believe it's a regex match on the name, so this should be possible.
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL at the "what to expect" section.  I believe I've got it operating how we discussed.  If not, or if there are other tweaks needed please LMK.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
